trivia_bullets.text: Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK, released September 27, 2024) is a top-down horror expansion set inside a sentient mansion-plane ruled by the demon Valgavoth. The House draws victims from across the Multiverse via Omenpaths; Unwilling Vessel depicts the horror of possession within that context. [Scryfall], [Wizards Planeswalker's Guide to Duskmourn]

Eerie is DSK's enchantmentfall variant: an ability word on creatures that triggers whenever an enchantment you control enters OR whenever you fully unlock a Room. Rooms are double-faced enchantment permanents unique to DSK; one card can trigger an Eerie ability twice. Wizards noted Eerie was deliberately designed so a single Room card could trigger it twice, rewarding players who build around enchantment density. [Wizards Duskmourn Mechanics article]

Unwilling Vessel (3/2 Vigilance Human Wizard, {2}{U}) accumulates possession counters via Eerie triggers and converts them into an X/X blue Spirit token with flying on death — a delayed payoff structure that rewards enchantment-heavy builds. Its EDHREC rank of 16,282 among DSK cards reflects modest Commander adoption. The card has no flavor text. [Scryfall]

The 'possession counter' counter name on Unwilling Vessel is a named counter type introduced in DSK — Scryfall confirms the counter is specifically named 'possession' not a generic +1/+1 counter, tying the mechanic directly to the horror-possession flavor of the plane. [Scryfall oracle_text]
